hazard.summary: A shared repository-level upload limit on GitHub Actions Cache was treated as a local retry concern while cache thrashing and tiering discussions were mixed into the same problem. The result is that per-call retry behavior can remain incorrect even if write volume is reduced architecturally.
signals: GitHub staff imposed 200 cache entry uploads per minute per repository
429 responses include Retry-After and should not be retried before that time
GitHub explicitly recommended exponential backoff and bounded retries
maintainers discussed multi-level caching as a possible fix
cache thrashing was identified separately from 429 handling
shared limit applies per repository rather than per process or per upload

constraints: Shared upstream limits must be coordinated across the whole repository workload, not handled only by per-call local retries.
Retry-After must take precedence over locally calculated backoff.
Rate-limit correctness and cache-volume/thrashing behavior must be treated as separate control problems.
Retries against shared service limits must be bounded by attempts or max elapsed time and emit observable diagnostics.
verification: Simulate GitHub Actions Cache 429 responses with Retry-After and verify uploads do not retry before the header delay.
Verify concurrent upload workers do not independently retry into the same shared repository limit without coordination.
Verify retry loops terminate after bounded attempts or max elapsed time.
Verify cache tiering or local-directory buffering does not substitute for correct 429 handling semantics.
notes: This receipt is valuable because it separates two failure classes that were initially blended together: shared-limit compliance and cache thrashing. Multi-level caching may reduce write volume, but it does not automatically implement correct Retry-After semantics or shared-limit coordination.

constraints: separate protocol correctness from architectural volume reduction
anti_patterns: per-call retry under shared repository-level limits
using architecture changes to avoid fixing 429 semantics
mixing quota thrash and rate-limit correctness into one fix
